intro: Kotlin dramatically reduces Java boilerplate while remaining fully JVM-compatible. Java's verbose getter/setter POJOs collapse into Kotlin `data class` declarations, `Optional<T>` is replaced by Kotlin's built-in null-safety operators (`?.`, `?:`, `!!`), anonymous inner classes become lambdas, and Java's checked exceptions simply disappear. The same logic that takes 50 lines of Java often fits in 15 lines of idiomatic Kotlin.

diffs.from: Full class/field/getter/setter boilerplate for every model.
diffs.to: data class gives equals/hashCode/copy/toString in one line.

diffs.from: All references nullable. NullPointerException at runtime.
diffs.to: T vs T? enforced by compiler. Safe-call (?.) and Elvis (?:) operators.
Coroutines: suspend, launch, async, flow — lightweight and composable.

diffs.from: Streams API (verbose). Anonymous inner classes for lambdas (pre-8).
diffs.to: Collection extensions (filter, map, flatMap) directly on any Iterable.

examples.title: Java POJO → Kotlin data class
examples.description: 25 lines of Java boilerplate collapses to a single Kotlin data class declaration.

examples.title: Stream chain → Kotlin collection extensions
examples.description: Java's Stream API becomes Kotlin's built-in collection functions — no collect() needed.

tips.title: POJO → data class
tips.body: Every Java POJO (fields + getters + equals/hashCode) becomes a Kotlin data class. Remove all getters — Kotlin properties are accessed directly: product.name, not product.getName().

tips.title: Optional<T> → nullable T?
tips.body: Replace Optional<T> with T?. Replace .orElse(default) with ?: default (Elvis operator). Replace .ifPresent{} with ?.let{}.

tips.title: CompletableFuture → coroutines
tips.body: Add kotlinx.coroutines. Mark async methods suspend fun. Replace thenApply with direct sequential code inside a coroutine scope. Launch with viewModelScope.launch or runBlocking.

tips.title: Switch → when
tips.body: Java switch becomes Kotlin when. It's an expression: val label = when (code) { 1 -> "one" 2 -> "two" else -> "other" }. No fall-through.
